draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer scannable contact cards over dense contact tables for quick reference and relationship context
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs. key metrics — whether CRM surfaces information as summarized cards or comprehensive tables
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick visual scanning, relationship context, and card-based UI patterns for contact management
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers data density, comparison rows, and table-based layouts for client information

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer scannable contact cards over dense contact tables for quick reference and relationship context
evidence.implication: User values quick visual scanning, relationship context, and card-based UI patterns for contact management

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to CRM interfaces that feel human and inviting rather than cold and corporate
facade.label: Warm, approachable
facade.acceptImplies: Future probes should explore softer typography, earth tones, rounded corners, and conversational copy that reduces friction in daily use
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward cooler, more minimal aesthetics—perhaps sharp corners, high contrast, and professional distance that signals efficiency over comfort

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer approachable, gentle aesthetics that feel less corporate and more human-centered
facade.axisTargeted: Color warmth and form softness vs. corporate sharpness
facade.label: Soft pastels and curves
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward warm, rounded, non-threatening design language — suggests CRM should feel like a helpful friend, not enterprise software
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clarity through contrast, modern minimalism, or professional edge — suggests design should prioritize efficiency signals and visual hierarchy over comfort

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a persistent left sidebar for navigation with client/project data displayed as cards in the main area, balancing quick access to tools with scannable content chunks.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure and content grouping (sidebar vs tabs; cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with Cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent, always-visible navigation and prefers chunked, visual content organization. Likely to accept: dense sidebars, card-based dashboards, and modular layouts.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer top navigation, tab-based organization, or list-heavy layouts. Suggests they want full-width workspaces or linear, sequential content flows.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer immediate visual feedback when entering client data rather than waiting for page refreshes or manual submissions
facade.label: Instant form updates
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should give real-time field validation, auto-save without delays, and instant UI changes as users type—reducing cognitive load and increasing trust in the system
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM can use traditional form submission patterns with deliberate pauses, confirmation steps, or batch updates—prioritizing stability and explicit user control over perceived speed

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to friendly, approachable language that feels like talking to a peer rather than corporate jargon
facade.label: Casual and conversational
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers warm, relatable tone—uses 'you' and 'let's', avoids formal bureau-speak, embraces personality in UI copy and messaging
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers polished professionalism—clear, measured, authoritative language that signals competence and trustworthiness over familiarity

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool or a supportive companion for solo work
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style (focused vs. inspired)
Whether user prefers numbers/metrics front-and-center vs. narrative summaries and insights—reveals if they think in data or stories when managing their business
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals pace preference and whether they value speed or thoughtfulness in their tools

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool or a supportive companion for solo work
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style (focused vs. inspired)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers numbers/metrics front-and-center vs. narrative summaries and insights—reveals if they think in data or stories when managing their business
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals pace preference and whether they value speed or thoughtfulness in their tools
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need chronological visualization of project deadlines and milestones to manage competing client timelines, rather than purely contact-based organization
facade.axisTargeted: Information structure — chronological vs relational organization
facade.label: Timeline view for projects
facade.acceptImplies: User prioritizes temporal workflow management and milestone tracking; CRM should surface deadline pressure and project momentum
facade.rejectImplies: User views CRM primarily as a contact/relationship tool; timeline data belongs in separate project management tool, not in core CRM interface

draft.title: Freelancer CRM
draft.summary: A lightweight client and project management tool designed for independent freelancers to track leads, manage projects, and maintain client relationships.
